questions.scenario: A health endpoint returns 200 before the app has connected to the database or loaded required secrets. What risk does this create?

options.text: Azure may send traffic to a copy that can answer the health path but fails real checkout requests.
The app will never scale out.

questions.explanation: Health checks should prove enough readiness for the traffic the service will receive. A shallow check can hide dependency failures.

questions.scenario: The team wants to change the health endpoint for `devpolaris-orders-api`. The reviewer wants the check to prove real readiness and to understand how Azure reacts when it fails. Which facts should be included when reviewing a health check change?

options.text: The path Azure checks and what the application does on that path.
Whether the check covers required dependencies or only process liveness.
How failed checks affect traffic or revision health.
The color of the portal status icon only.

questions.explanation: A health check is an operating contract. The team should know what it proves and how Azure acts on it.
